title: The Greenwood Boys
visual_consistency: Recurring fictional pop group: five generic young men in their early 20s with friendly expressive faces, neat 1960s mod haircuts, matching forest-green jackets over white shirts, dark trousers, and simple stage microphones; they must not resemble any real celebrity. Recurring small-town fans are excited teenagers and young adults in safe orderly groups with scarves and coats. Keep the modest English town, train station, local workers' club stage, and crowd-control mood connected. Use one single uninterrupted cinematic illustration per frame, no comic panels, no split screen, no collage, no readable text.

frames.prompt: A small English town preparing for a visiting pop singer group: a tour announcement board or poster-like display in a town square, with excited young people noticing the news and pointing at the schedule area. The board should feel like it announces a concert tour, but it must contain no readable text, no letters, no captions, no visible words.

A modest small-town train station in daylight, with young fans waiting in orderly groups near the platform before a pop group arrives by train. The mood is excited but safe and organized, with a clear sense of anticipation. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.

Evening performance at an old-style local workers' club or community hall: a generic pop singer group performing on a small stage for a cheerful audience. The venue should look local and modest, not a giant stadium, and the performers should not resemble any real celebrity. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.

Police officers and event staff calmly guiding an excited crowd into orderly lines outside a local club after a popular performance. The scene is about crowd control and keeping order, not crime or danger; people are smiling and moving safely. No text, no letters, no captions, no readable signs, no visible words.
